core_rule: Decisions that shape future work must survive the session where they were made.

decisions.title: GitHub Is The Source Of Truth
decisions.decision: GitHub (RodbotCC/CCAgent, branch main) is the durable source of truth for the project. The local file tree is the working copy.
decisions.context: The project needs continuity across agents, tools, MCP servers, local machines, and future sessions. Local-only state and chat history are not durable enough.
decisions.rationale: GitHub gives the project shared, versioned, inspectable memory. It lets other agents and tools connect through Git instead of relying on the user's local machine.
consequences: Important project memory belongs in repo files.
Ledgers must be committed.
Direct GitHub writes must consider unpushed local work.
The repo IS the memory of the build.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not move core continuity back into chat-only or local-only memory without explicit review.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if GitHub stops being accessible or another versioned source-of-truth system replaces it.

decisions.title: FileTree Orchestration Over RAG Memory
decisions.decision: The project uses FileTree orchestration as its primary memory and coordination model — not RAG / vector memory.
decisions.context: The user wants durable, inspectable, directory-aware continuity.
decisions.rationale: FileTree orchestration gives agents explicit, versioned, local context. Easier to audit, rebuild, hand off, and reason about than vague retrieval.
consequences: Important memory belongs in files.
Directories should become Boxes when meaningful.
Local context lives near files it governs.
Pieces.app stays as secondary memory backend, not primary.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not replace Box / local-file continuity with hidden memory without review.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if a hybrid retrieval layer is added as a helper while keeping file tree as source of truth.

decisions.title: Client Boxes Are Canonical Client Truth
decisions.decision: Auto/Client Boxes/<Name>/ is the canonical per-client memory and operating substrate. The Boxes page and orchestrator state are read / render / generation layers over that substrate.
decisions.context: There was confusion about whether the boxes UI/page or Auto/Boxes umbrella was the actual client truth.
decisions.rationale: Client-specific complexity should live inside the client's own Box. One client's constraints should not force global automation rewrites.
consequences: Client-specific edits happen in the relevant Client Box.
Boxes page is never canonical client truth.
Allowed-To-Know layer (PROB-001) will live inside each Client Box.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not make UI or generated dashboard state the canonical source for client truth without explicit migration.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if a database-backed canonical client layer is intentionally introduced.

decisions.title: Seven-Day Plans Are Strategy Drafts, Not Send Licenses
decisions.decision: A seven-day plan is a strategy draft. It does not authorize sending by itself. Reply received -> plan is stale.
decisions.context: Some plans were written before guardrails were fully enforced. The Brenda & Steve audit revealed plans with stale dates, risky commitment language, and enrichment leakage.
decisions.rationale: Plans age. Replies, transcripts, state sweeps, and updated guardrails can invalidate a previously good plan.
alternatives_considered: Treat plan text as send authority
Require manual rewrite before every move
consequences: Scheduled fires must treat plans as input, not authority.
If a lead replies, plan is stale.
Plan audits check for stale dates, risky commitments, enrichment leakage.
Brenda's plan now opens with Audit-cleaned line — that's the template.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not allow automation to send directly from plan text without guardrail validation.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit if a formal plan compiler / validator is built that enforces all gates before execution.

decisions.title: Risky Sales Moves Require Isolated Approval
decisions.decision: Risky sales moves may be suggested by automation, but they require explicit isolated approval before customer-facing send.
risky_move_examples: strong claims not grounded in comms
decisions.context: The Brenda & Steve fee-waiver move may have been strategically correct, but it was approved in a rushed batch context and did not receive enough isolated risk friction.
decisions.rationale: Good sales judgment and safe automation are not the same thing. A human should explicitly own financial, pricing, scope, or risky personalization commitments.
alternatives_considered: Allow risky moves inside normal batch approval
consequences: Risky moves should become explicit approval cards.
Approval cards should name the risk clearly.
Batch approval should not hide financial / scope commitments.
Approval UI specifics still under review (see decisions_needing_review).
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not let risky commitments ship as ordinary follow-up copy without explicit approval.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit after approval UI supports isolated risk confirmations robustly.

decisions.title: Boxes Page Is Display Layer, Not Canonical Truth
decisions.decision: The boxes route / page is a UI read / display surface over Client Boxes, Staff Boxes, and orchestrator state. It does not own canonical client truth.
decisions.context: The sitemap says the Boxes page reads Auto/Client Boxes, Auto/Staff Boxes, Auto/orchestrator/state, and exposes a single-surface dossier view. That could visually imply ownership if not documented.
decisions.rationale: UI should make truth visible, not become truth accidentally.
consequences: Fix client truth in Client Boxes.
Fix generated state through sources / generators.
Page changes update sitemap (DEC-007).
Boxes page should communicate source freshness.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not write client truth into the Boxes page layer without source-of-truth migration.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit if a database or app-native canonical Box store replaces file-based Client Boxes.

decisions.title: Page-Asset Sitemap Is Mandatory Done Gate For Page Changes
decisions.decider: Existing project policy / accepted into ledger system
decisions.decision: Any task that changes a page, route behavior, or page data binding is not complete until page_asset_sitemap.md is updated.
decisions.context: The Comeketo Page-Asset Source Of Truth already defines this mandatory Done Gate. Formalized into the ledger system 2026-04-28.
decisions.rationale: Page behavior and data ownership drift quickly if not recorded.
consequences: UI / page work includes sitemap update in same unit of work.
Asset / Widget Map links to sitemap rather than duplicating it.
Audits can fail page work if sitemap is stale.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not remove this Done Gate without replacing it with an equal or stronger page ownership system.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if Page Ledgers or an automated page map supersede the sitemap.

decisions.title: Build Ledgers One At A Time
decisions.decision: The ledger system is designed and implemented one ledger at a time. The user signals (typically by typing P) to proceed to the next.
decisions.context: The ledger system is large (~22 planned ledgers). Implementing it all at once would be too broad and would create shallow files.
decisions.rationale: One-ledger-at-a-time keeps focus, creates rhythm, and lets the user hand clean implementation outlines to the building agent.
consequences: Each ledger gets a dedicated outline at /Ledger Drafts/.
Implementation evolves without overwhelming repo.
Build order tracked in INDEX.md.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not attempt a massive all-ledgers rewrite unless the user explicitly changes workflow.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit after first wave of ledgers is implemented.

decisions.title: Triad Spine: Box + Ledger + Sub-agent
decisions.decision: The architectural spine of Comeketo Agent is the Box + Ledger + Sub-agent triad. Anything with state that learns or needs updating gets all three.

triad_legs.Box: the unit of state — Client Boxes, Staff Boxes, Coworker records, eventually any state-bearing entity
triad_legs.Ledger: the legible memory of that Box — project-wide ledgers under LEDGERS/; per-Box ledgers stamp from LEDGERS/LOCAL_TEMPLATE/BOX_LEDGER_TEMPLATE.md
triad_legs.Sub-agent: the operator that reads / updates the Box and its Ledger — canonical config in /Subagent Boxes/<name>/; runnable form in CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/

decisions.standing_rule: Legibility is more important than the build itself. Code without a corresponding Ledger entry is debt to repay. A Ledger reader should understand the system without opening code.
decisions.jake_exact_words: For everything that we build we cannot be lazy about the legibility. I am actually more concerned about the legibility than the build.
decisions.context: After the plumbing-first attempt was scrapped under demo pressure (see COMM-2026-04-28-008), the architectural spine wasn't formally named. On 2026-04-29 the triad was codified as the rule organizing everything stateful.
decisions.rationale: Ledgers without Boxes are floating memory. Boxes without Ledgers are illegible state. Sub-agents without either are operators with no memory. Skipping any leg produces a system that cannot continue itself across agents.
consequences: Before designing anything stateful, ask: Where is the Box? Where is the Ledger? Where will the Sub-agent live?
The triad is currently lopsided (more Boxes than Ledgers, more Ledgers than Sub-agents).
Per-Box ledgers stamp from LEDGERS/LOCAL_TEMPLATE/BOX_LEDGER_TEMPLATE.md.
Steward sub-agents follow the global_ledger_steward pattern.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not propose architectural patterns that bypass any leg of the triad. If a system has state, it gets all three.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit only if a fundamentally different state-management primitive emerges (would also trigger DEC-003 review).

decisions.title: Three-Phase Build Discipline (A -> B -> C)
decisions.decision: Build order, in this exact sequence: Phase A finish all Ledgers (currently here) -> Phase B finish all Sub-agents -> Phase C build Subagent Boxes.

A.name: Ledgers
A.description: Ingest outline drafts from /Ledger Drafts/ one at a time, in user-confirmed order.

B.name: Sub-agents
B.description: Graduate the draft sub-agent packages at /Subagent Boxes/ into runnable app agents under CCAgentindex/agents/<name>/.

C.name: Subagent Boxes
C.description: Build Subagent Boxes (boxes of ledgers controlled by sub-agent configurations). The triad becomes operational.

decisions.context: After the rebuild, the triad needed to be filled in a specific order to avoid producing partial systems.
decisions.rationale: Ledgers without sub-agents are static memory. Sub-agents without ledgers have nowhere to write. Subagent Boxes require both prior layers.
consequences: Confirm phase before proposing structure.
Phase A pattern: build the ledger only, note sub-agent home as placeholder.
PROB-016 blocked on Phase A + Phase B completion.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not skip phases. Do not parallelize without explicit user approval.

decisions.title: TCL/GL Update Discipline (Same Unit Of Work)
decisions.decision: Cold-starting agents read TCL and GL first. If those are stale, every agent that boots from them is stale. Therefore: whoever does the work also updates the orientation surface in the same unit of work — not after, not later.
required_updates_per_change: Update header Last updated dates on TCL and GL when applicable.
Append to TCL Section 3 Recent Meaningful Changes for any non-trivial change.
Update TCL Section 1 Current Snapshot if anything structural changed.
Update GL Section 12 Recently Changed for major moves.
Update GL Section 2 Current World State if a structural fact changed.
Append to CCAgentindex/_ledger/activity.jsonl for any non-trivial delegation.
decisions.context: Failure mode happened during a Cowork -> Claude Code -> Cowork handoff on 2026-04-29: a Cowork session read a TCL written ~24 hours earlier and had no idea about that day's catch-up work.
decisions.jake_exact_words: The ledger is more important than the actual work. Literally everything needs to be ledgered.
decisions.rationale: The fix is not smarter agents — it's discipline. Orientation surfaces ARE the agent. If they're stale, the agent is stale.
consequences: Every non-trivial work session ends with a TCL/GL update.
Header dates are bumped accurately and frequently.
Cold-starting agents can trust TCL/GL as current.
global_ledger_steward verifies drift but does not replace this discipline.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Do not adopt patterns that defer ledger updates to 'later' or to a separate commit. Same unit of work, every time.
decisions.review_trigger: Does not expire. This is the meta-rule that keeps every other ledger usable.

decisions.title: Audit Ledger Removed From Build Queue
decisions.decision: The Audit Ledger is removed from the build queue. Audit-shaped findings will land in Open Problems, Decisions, Communications, or per-Box ledgers as appropriate.
decisions.context: After Communications Ledger landed and OPL had been live for a day, Jake observed that audit findings naturally distribute across the three sibling ledgers + per-Box ledgers. A dedicated Audit Ledger would duplicate coverage already provided.
decisions.jake_exact_words: I don't think we're ever going to need that. It's basically covered in the other things.
decisions.rationale: Standalone Audit Ledger would create three failure modes: coverage overlap, discoverability cost, maintenance overhead.
alternatives_considered: Build a thin Audit Ledger that cross-references
consequences: INDEX.md row marked out-of-scope (2026-04-29).
GLOBAL_LEDGER Section 8 Audit row marked similarly.
Build order item 4 (in INDEX) struck through.
Audit-pattern findings route to Open Problems / Decisions / Communications / per-Box.
Brenda audit pattern continues to work — routes findings into existing ledgers.
Draft outline at /Ledger Drafts/# Audit Ledger.txt stays as reference; not deleted.
decisions.do_not_undo_casually: Confidence is medium because this decision is hours old. If a future agent finds audit findings genuinely don't fit any of the four homes, it's worth re-opening this question. Don't reverse without that evidence.
decisions.review_trigger: Revisit if a future audit pattern doesn't fit Open Problems / Decisions / Communications / per-Box; or if audit-shaped reporting becomes a frequent operator-facing need (compliance, regulatory); or if volume makes per-ledger distribution painful.

decisions_needing_review.question: What exact UI / process should isolate approval for risky moves?
decisions_needing_review.current_working_assumption: Risky moves become explicit approval cards requiring specific confirmation. Format and storage are not yet specified.
needs_decision_on: wording (what the card says about the risk)
approval logging (where the approval is recorded)
where approval lives (Client Box? _ledger/? dedicated approvals table?)
how approval attaches to future sends (signed token? reference ID? audit field?)
decisions_needing_review.suggested_timeline: When the approval surface gets concrete UI work, this becomes a real Decision.

visuals.name: decision_dependency_map
visuals.purpose: Graph of how decisions depend on each other

visuals.name: decision_timeline
visuals.purpose: Chronological view of all active decisions
